CSCE 656 Syllabus

Section 600 (42432)

Computers and New Media

Spring 2026 - College Station

Course Information

Meeting Times:  Meeting Type: LEC

Meeting Days: MWF

Start Time: 09:10AM

End Time: 10:00AM

Start Date: 01/12/2026

End Date: 05/05/2026

Meeting Location:  EABB 106

Credit Hours:  3

Instructor Details

Frank Shipman

Email:  shipman@tamu.edu

Office Location:  PETE 335

Phone:  979-862-3216

Office Hours

To Be Determined

Catalog Description

Computers and New Media. (3-0). Credit 3. This class investigates the potential and

realized impact of computers in the design of new media, explores the variety of

relationships between authors and readers of interactive materials, and explores

the influence of media design and content expressed. Prerequisite: Graduate

classification.

relationships between authors and readers of interactive materials, and explores

the influence of media design and content expressed. Prerequisite: Graduate

classification.

Course Prerequisites

Prerequisite/Corequisite(s):  Graduate classification.

Course Learning Outcomes

Upon completion of this course, the learner will be able to:

analyze characteristics of computer-based media and anticipate likely reactions to

them

design software features that support immersion, agency, and transformation

describe techniques for creating computer characters and narrative control

develop software for the manipulation/use of audio and/or video

describe core differences between physical and intellectual property

assess characteristics of software licenses, software patents, and digital rights

management software

design, implement and evaluate software supporting the creation and/or

consumption of content in specific settings

Special Course Designation

management software

design, implement and evaluate software supporting the creation and/or

consumption of content in specific settings

Special Course Designation

None

Grading Policy

Homework: 10%

Team project: 35%

Readings write-ups: 35%

Survey paper: 10%

Attendance (via sign-in sheet): 10%

Grades will be on a 10 point scale: 90-100 = A, 80-89 = B, 70-79 = C, 60-69 = D, and

<60 = F.

Late Work Policy

Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not

considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy ( Student Rule 7 ).

<60 = F.

Late Work Policy

Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not

considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy ( Student Rule 7 ).

Course Specific Late Work Policy

Write-ups late policy:

50% is immediately deducted from your grade if turned in late. 5% more is deducted for

every day late up to a maximum of one week after the original due date. For example, if

a write up is turned in between 48-72 hours late, the highest grade it could receive is

40%. Do the reading and turn in the write-ups before class.

Other assignments late policy:

10% is deducted per day late up to a maximum of one week after the original due date.

Course Schedule

week 1: introductions, history of media studies

week 2: designing software for immersion

week 3: designing software for agency and transformation

week 4: interactive media and computer characters

week 5: interactive text and social media

week 2: designing software for immersion

week 3: designing software for agency and transformation

week 4: interactive media and computer characters

week 5: interactive text and social media

week 6: bridging physical and digital media

week 7: audio, music, and speech

week 8: recorded and authored video

week 9: games and computer-controlled characters

week 10: automatic narrative control

week 11: digital rights management

week 12: property rights and issues

week 13: software licenses and intellectual property

week 14: evaluating policy effects

week 15: student project presentations

Significant Due Dates:

Assignment 1 due January 26

Assignment 2 due February 16

Survey paper due May 1

Final project presentations due April 20-28

Final project reports due May 1

smaller project activities will be due once per week and readings write-ups

will be due on average twice per week
